name: gen-env
description: Creates, updates, or reviews a project's gen-env command for running multiple isolated instances on localhost. Handles instance identity, port allocation, data isolation, browser state separation, and cleanup.
qualityReason: Clear purpose, specific local dev use
capabilities: Generates environment configurations for multiple isolated project instances on localhost
Allocates unique ephemeral ports from range 49152-65535 to prevent conflicts
Creates Docker Compose project names for data and network isolation
Generates subdomain-based URLs using *.localhost for browser cookie separation
Creates lockfiles to maintain port consistency across runs
Reviews existing gen-env implementations against best practices checklist
author: 0xBigBoss
securitySummary: Static analysis detected patterns in documentation files (ADVANCED_PATTERNS.md, IMPLEMENTATION.md, SKILL.md) containing shell command examples and environment variable usage. These are educational examples of legitimate development patterns (Docker Compose, port allocation, environment generation). No malicious intent, data exfiltration, or unauthorized credential access found. The skill is a local development environment management tool with standard devops functionality.
valueStatement: Running multiple instances of the same project on localhost causes port conflicts, data corruption, and browser state mixing. This skill generates environment configurations that isolate each instance with unique ports, Docker resources, and browser domains.
limitations: Does not execute Docker commands - only generates configuration files
Does not modify existing Docker resources or containers
Requires direnv or similar tool to source generated environment files
Does not handle HTTPS or SSL certificate generation

useCases.title: Run multiple feature branches simultaneously
useCases.description: Developers working on multiple feature branches can run each instance with its own workspace name, ports, and data without conflicts.

useCases.title: Test different versions in isolation
useCases.description: QA engineers can test version A and version B of the same application side by side with completely separate databases and browser sessions.

useCases.title: Review existing gen-env implementations
useCases.description: Maintainers can validate that their project's gen-env script follows all best practices for instance isolation and cleanup.

promptTemplates.title: Generate basic gen-env script
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a gen-env command script for my project that generates environment configurations for running multiple isolated instances. The script should accept a --name argument for workspace identity, allocate ports from ephemeral range 49152-65535, and output a .localnet.env file with COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME, unique ports, and *.localhost host URLs.
promptTemplates.scenario: Generate a new gen-env script from scratch

promptTemplates.title: Add port conflict handling
promptTemplates.prompt: Add port conflict detection and handling to my existing gen-env script. When a requested port is in use, it should try the next available port with a 100ms timeout, and warn if no ports are available.

promptTemplates.title: Implement browser isolation
promptTemplates.prompt: Update my gen-env script to generate browser-isolated URLs using *.localhost subdomains. Each instance should get a unique hostname like feature-x.localhost so cookies and localStorage stay separate between instances.

promptTemplates.title: Review gen-env implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my project's gen-env implementation at [file path] against the checklist: validates workspace name, generates COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME, allocates ephemeral ports, creates lockfile, and supports surgical cleanup. List any gaps and suggest fixes.
promptTemplates.scenario: Audit existing gen-env for best practices

bestPractices: Always validate workspace names (alphanumeric + dashes, max 63 chars for DNS compatibility)
Store allocated ports in a lockfile so subsequent runs reuse the same ports for consistency
Use ephemeral port range 49152-65535 to avoid conflicts with well-known services
Generate *.localhost hosts for browser isolation instead of different localhost ports
antiPatterns: Using hardcoded localhost URLs (http://localhost:3000) which share cookies between instances
Omitting COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME which causes Docker to use directory name and risk conflicts
Using docker compose down -v without project name which removes all instances indiscriminately
Sharing the same database volume across instances causing data corruption

faq.question: What ports should I use for my instances?
faq.answer: Use ports from the ephemeral range 49152-65535. These ports are not assigned to well-known services and are safe for dynamic allocation. Allocate unique ports per service (postgres, redis, api, web) for each instance.

faq.question: How does browser isolation work with *.localhost?
faq.answer: Chrome and Edge automatically treat *.localhost as 127.0.0.1. Different subdomains (main.localhost vs feature-x.localhost) maintain separate cookies and localStorage even on the same port, solving the isolation problem that different ports on localhost cannot fix.

faq.question: What is COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME and why does it matter?
faq.answer: COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME controls the prefix for all Docker resources (containers, networks, volumes, services). Setting it explicitly ensures each instance gets isolated resources with predictable names like localnet-feature-x_postgres_data.

faq.question: How do I handle port conflicts between instances?
faq.answer: Check port availability before assignment using a short timeout (100ms). If a port is in use, try the next port in the sequence. Store allocated ports in a lockfile so the same instance always gets the same ports on subsequent runs.

faq.question: What should I commit to version control?
faq.answer: Commit the gen-env script itself and any configuration templates. Add .localnet.env and .gen-env.lock to .gitignore since these are instance-specific generated files that should not be shared.

faq.question: How do I integrate gen-env with my existing workflow?
faq.answer: Use direnv with a .envrc file that sources the generated .localnet.env. Add the gen-env script directory to PATH. Run gen-env --name <workspace> before starting Docker Compose, and gen-env --clean when done.
